topics.title: Substance Use, Craving, and Adolescent Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on substance use disorders and related behaviors such as craving, reward processing, and alcohol or cannabis use. It also highlights adolescent brain development and functional connectivity patterns linked to vulnerability and initiation of use.

keywords: cortico subcortical mechanisms of dynamic music reward processing
music and emotion in functional connectivity
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Music Reward and Skill
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music listening, participation, and training shape reward, memory, emotion, and sensorimotor brain systems. It also includes studies of musical skill retention, neural decoding, and developmental or structural correlates of musical experience.

keywords: foundation models identify eeg biomarkers for alzheimer 's
nonlinear eeg complexity reveal sleep relate neural signatures
topics.title: EEG Biomarkers and Sleep-State Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on using EEG-based biomarkers and modern learning methods to characterize sleep-related neural signatures, vigilance state, and sleep disorders. It also includes applications to Alzheimer’s and aging, highlighting biomarker discovery from wearable and multimodal signals.

topics.title: Olfactory Processing and Body Odor Responses
topics.description: This cluster centers on olfactory perception, including body odor, smell identification, and congenital anosmia. It also highlights brain responses and neural mechanisms underlying olfactory dysfunction and odor-related processing.

keywords: homeostatic and hedonic neural correlates of food memory relate
topics.title: Neural Control of Eating and Obesity
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural correlates of eating behavior, food choice, and appetite regulation across obesity, eating disorders, and related patient populations. It also includes studies linking functional connectivity and dynamic brain states to food-related cognition and decision-making.

topics.title: Brain Markers of Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on chronic pain conditions and their associated brain mechanisms, including functional connectivity, structural asymmetry, morphology, and perfusion changes. It also spans specific pain disorders such as trigeminal neuralgia, fibromyalgia, osteoarthritis, and primary headache.

keywords: functional connectivity pathways support fear avoidance processes
topics.title: Brain and spinal mechanisms of pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on how pain is represented and modulated across the brain and spinal cord, including experimental, evoked, and chronic pain states. It also highlights fear-avoidance, catastrophizing, and connectivity-based mechanisms linked to pain sensitivity and relief.

topics.title: Hormonal Effects on Brain Structure and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how hormonal states and transitions across the lifespan relate to brain structure, connectivity, and arousal systems. It includes menstrual cycle effects, pregnancy, menopause, and related hormone-sensitive neural adaptations.

keywords: prediction of brain responses reveal emotional effects in sentence processing
topics.title: Neural and Language Model Prediction of Sentences
topics.description: This cluster centers on predicting brain responses during sentence and narrative processing, especially emotional and social meaning in language. It also includes multimodal and large language model approaches for decoding and cross-subject prediction in neuroimaging.

topics.title: Federated Neuroimaging Data Integration
topics.description: This cluster focuses on federated approaches for integrating cross-border neuroimaging and linked datasets under legal and governance constraints. It also emphasizes validation, uncertainty quantification, and machine learning models for epidemiological and clinical neuroscience research.

keywords: genetic basis of human hippocampal organization genome wide analysis of multimodal imaging traits
topics.title: Genetic and Imaging Bases of Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how genetic and epigenetic factors relate to brain structure, organization, and dynamic brain states across multimodal imaging measures. It also includes analytic approaches such as genome-wide analysis, fine mapping, and hidden Markov models used to infer brain architecture and variation.

topics.title: Sex Differences Across Brain Development and Ageing
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain anatomy, structure, and cortical morphometry vary across the human lifespan and neurodevelopmental trajectory. It also includes comparative work on sex differences, gender diversity, and related subgroup effects.

keywords: collaborative infrastructure around shared tool interfaces for neuroimaging
federated neuroimaging data management platform for multisite studies
topics.title: Scalable Reproducible Neuroimaging Infrastructure
topics.description: The cluster centers on infrastructure and standards for managing, converting, and sharing neuroimaging data across sites. It emphasizes scalable, reproducible workflows, quality assessment, and collaborative tool integration for large-scale studies.

keywords: facilitate open and reproducible meta analysis with automation
topics.title: Open Science and Automated Meta-analysis in Neuroscience
topics.description: This cluster centers on open science practices, data sharing, and policy efforts that support reproducible neuroscience research. It also highlights automated meta-analysis tools and network-mapping approaches used to organize and review the field.

topics.title: Ketamine Effects on Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on ketamine administration and acute challenge studies examining altered perception, dose-dependent pharmacodynamics, and changes in neural information dynamics. It links functional connectome and molecular connectivity alterations to therapeutic response in healthy adults and major depression.

topics.title: Psilocybin and Cortical Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how psilocybin and related psychedelics alter large-scale brain organization, connectivity, and cortical dynamics. It highlights network reorganization, entropy, frequency-specific effects, and spatiotemporal signatures observed in human brain activity.

topics.title: Neural correlates and modulation of tinnitus
topics.description: This cluster centers on tinnitus as a phantom auditory perception, including its neural correlates, subtype differences, and factors linked to loudness or suppression. It also highlights brain and electrophysiological findings, such as cortical modulation, resting-state EEG, and structural or neurochemical alterations.

keywords: inclusive unsedated brain mri protocol for neurodiverse children with behaviours of concern
topics.title: Motion-Friendly Pediatric MRI Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on approaches to reduce head motion and improve MRI feasibility in children, especially neurodiverse and behaviorally challenging pediatric groups. It includes training protocols, virtual reality tools, and minimal-motion systems for more stable neuroimaging data collection.

topics.title: Epilepsy Networks and Seizure Localization
topics.description: These studies focus on identifying epileptogenic and seizure onset zones, especially in focal and pediatric epilepsy, using invasive and noninvasive recordings. They emphasize altered connectivity, excitation-inhibition dynamics, and network-level organization relevant to epilepsy characterization and surgical targeting.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Network Changes After Tremor Interventions
topics.description: This cluster centers on essential tremor and related movement disorders, emphasizing focused ultrasound and DBS effects on cerebellothalamic and sensorimotor networks. It also highlights functional MRI, connectivity fingerprints, and longitudinal biomarkers for predicting and explaining durable treatment outcomes.

keywords: causal evidence for distinct functional hubs
steerable multi target temporal interference stimulation
topics.title: Personalized Brain Stimulation for Targeted Network Modulation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on optimizing noninvasive brain stimulation approaches to engage specific neural circuits and functional hubs. It includes personalized montage design, coil sequence selection, and emerging modalities such as temporal interference and transcranial ultrasound.

topics.title: Personalized Network Effects in DBS
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation, especially subthalamic nucleus approaches in Parkinson disease, and how network-level mechanisms shape treatment response. It emphasizes connectomic and computational methods for personalization, prediction, and interpretation of functional and neuropsychiatric outcomes.

keywords: personalized fc- e field guide precision tms fmri
targeting approach for network level brain stimulation
topics.title: Personalized Brain Stimulation and Cognitive Control
topics.description: This cluster centers on personalized neuromodulation approaches and network-level brain stimulation to modulate cognitive control and related brain activity. It emphasizes multimodal imaging, connectivity-guided targeting, and stimulation effects on frontal and cingulate/insular networks.

keywords: tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation
tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation and cortical silent period duration
uncertainty aware open source bayesian motor threshold estimation tool
effect of epidural spinal stimulation on sensorimotor rhythms
topics.title: TMS and spinal stimulation methods in motor control
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial magnetic stimulation and related stimulation approaches for probing and localizing motor system function. It includes motor threshold estimation, coil orientation effects, paired-pulse protocols, and links between epidural spinal stimulation, motor imagery, and sensorimotor rhythms.

keywords: causal contribution of primary motor cortex
topics.title: Causal Brain Stimulation and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how transcranial stimulation methods are used to probe causal contributions of brain regions, especially the primary motor cortex, and their effects on connectivity and motor inhibition. It also includes studies combining stimulation with fMRI or ultrasound to assess rapid neuroplasticity and network-level responses.

keywords: deep learning base brain age prediction
topics.title: Deep Learning for Alzheimer MRI Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning and MRI-based approaches for Alzheimer disease progression, early detection, and dementia diagnosis. It also includes brain age prediction, explainable AI, and longitudinal prediction methods built from 3D brain imaging.

keywords: multimodal evidence for fast optical signals in visual cortex
topics.title: Multimodal Optical and Electromagnetic Brain Signals
topics.description: This cluster centers on evidence for fast optical signals and related hemodynamic activity measured with combined fNIR/NIRS approaches alongside EEG, MEG, and TMS recordings. The studies emphasize cortical responses during motor and visual tasks, including finger movement, manual dexterity, and postural transitions.

keywords: gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
functional and metabolic network signatures of
topics.title: Neurometabolic Network Organization in the Brain
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how metabolic and functional properties are organized across human brain networks, including gradient-like patterns, connectomic structure, and multimodal integration. It also highlights imaging approaches for mapping cortical metabolism and neurometabolic signatures in health and disease.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
long term abacus base mental calculation learning
topics.title: Neural Development in Math Learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental math abilities, especially dyscalculia, arithmetic fact retrieval, and performance changes across childhood and adolescence. It also includes how training and learning experiences reshape numerical brain networks and neural activation.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Language and Reading
topics.description: This cluster centers on language processing, speech, reading, and comprehension across development and clinical conditions such as dyslexia and aphasia. It also includes multilingual and child language studies using brain fmri and related neural measures.

topics.title: Autism subtypes and brain connectivity changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism and related developmental subgroups across childhood and adolescence, with emphasis on clinically distinct profiles and autistic traits. The abstracts highlight altered brain connectivity, network morphology, and EEG-related biomarkers as markers of heterogeneity in ASD.

keywords: the influence of visual perceptual load
topics.title: Attentional Control and Cognitive Engagement
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how attentional control is shaped by visual perceptual load and attention training techniques. It also includes physiological and behavioral measures such as eye tracking, pupillometric biomarkers, and skin conductance to assess task and cognitive engagement.

keywords: transient attentional events shape functional lateralization
topics.title: Naturalistic Movie fMRI and Brain Encoding
topics.description: These studies examine how naturalistic movie viewing and temporal content structure relate to brain encoding, default mode network engagement, and neural typicality. They also connect subjective aesthetic transitions, attentional events, and cross-species comparison to functional responses in humans and macaques.

topics.title: OCD Networks and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, highlighting frontostriatal and CSTC circuitry, brain activation, and compulsivity-related network alterations. It also includes treatment-related work on CBT, deep TMS, psychotherapy, and neural predictors of clinical response, especially in youth and other OCD subgroups.

topics.title: fMRI Reliability and High-Resolution Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on improving the reliability, reproducibility, and measurement quality of fMRI across repeat and multi-site settings. It also emphasizes high-resolution, multi-echo, and laminar processing approaches for more robust activation and model analyses.

topics.title: Retinotopic and Representational Visual Cortex Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping and modeling human visual cortex organization using retinotopy, fMRI decoding, and encoding approaches. It also includes work linking visual representations to artificial networks, natural scenes, and functional specialization across visual pathways.

keywords: optical rest state functional mri datum
topics.title: Functional MRI of Awake Social Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on functional MRI approaches used to study awake animals, including resting-state and task-related responses, brain mapping, and population-level dynamics. The abstracts emphasize attention, social interaction, and comparative work in mice and marmosets.

topics.title: Brain Development and Network Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging, histology, and graph-based analyses of brain connectivity and structural organization across development and recovery. It includes studies of normative maturation, adolescent and animal brain development, and network reorganization in vestibular and neuromotor conditions.

keywords: efficacy of personalized connectivity guide brain stimulation in treatment resistant depression
topics.title: Personalized Brain Stimulation for Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on individualized neuromodulation approaches for depression, especially treatment resistant depression, using connectivity, functional imaging, and stimulation targeting. It also includes neural markers and stimulation effects linked to antidepressant response and efficacy.

keywords: contextual functional connectomics of sex differences in cognition
social refinement of functional brain development
topics.title: Social Brain Dynamics and Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain network organization, functional development, and neural dynamics relate to social cognition and participation. It also includes work on sex differences, loneliness, and latent neural profiles across large cohorts and multimodal analyses.

topics.title: Genetic Links to Brain Structure and Psychopathology
topics.description: This cluster centers on genetically informed analyses of how polygenic risk and shared genetic architecture relate to human brain structure, cortical and neuroanatomical features, and structural vulnerability. It also links these findings to schizophrenia, severe mental illness, and other neuropsychiatric conditions, including negative symptom dimensions and brain aging.

keywords: developmental brain network trajectories differentiate resilience
topics.title: Brain Network Alterations in Psychosis Risk
topics.description: These studies focus on altered brain connectivity and network dynamics in schizophrenia and the psychosis spectrum, with emphasis on risk, resilience, and hallucination-related functional connectivity. They also highlight individual variability and developmental trajectories that may distinguish vulnerable from resilient outcomes.

topics.title: Psychosis Heterogeneity and Brain Circuitry
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and related psychosis-spectrum disorders, including early psychosis, bipolar disorder, and 22q11.2 deletion syndrome. It emphasizes heterogeneity, cognitive deficits, treatment response, and altered brain connectivity and circuits.

keywords: motor learning through imitation of fully visible
representational organization of hand actions across motor execution
the neural correlates of hand gesture planning and execution
topics.title: Action Observation and Grasping in Motor Control
topics.description: This cluster centers on how observing, imitating, and executing hand actions and grasps shape neural representations and motor control. It highlights the organization and reorganization of action-observation networks during hand gesture planning, grasping, and visuomotor learning.

topics.title: Ultra-High-Field Sensorimotor Representation Studies
topics.description: This cluster centers on ultra-high-field fMRI investigations of sensorimotor organization, including finger and body representations, proprioceptive processing, and primary motor/somatosensory mapping. Several abstracts also examine body ownership, spinal functional MRI, and representation changes during movement or stimulation.

keywords: association of age and psychopathology with alpha oscillatory
topics.title: EEG Markers of Age and Psychopathology
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG and broader brain electrophysiology measures used to study age-related change, lifespan differences, and psychopathology. It also includes work on brain age prediction and neural features such as alpha oscillations and aperiodic activity, often in naturalistic listening and conversational settings.

keywords: spatiotemporal development and functional parcellation of infant
topics.title: Infant Brain Network Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how functional brain networks and connectome organization emerge and change across fetal, neonatal, and early childhood periods, including preterm development. It emphasizes developmental trajectories, individual variability, and spatiotemporal patterns of infant brain maturation.

topics.title: Developmental Brain Network Reorganization
topics.description: Studies of how brain networks change across development, from infancy through adolescence and childhood. The cluster emphasizes maturation, individualized network organization, and developmental variation in functional connectivity.

keywords: cognitive load modulates phase base eeg brain state dynamics
topics.title: EEG Dynamics of Cognitive Control Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how EEG features and network interactions relate to cognitive control, working memory, and state-dependent brain dynamics. It emphasizes oscillatory, aperiodic, and multiscale markers of resting and task-evoked cognition.

topics.title: Hippocampal Dynamics in Episodic Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal contributions to encoding, recall, and episodic memory consolidation, including long-axis specialization and mismatch detection. It also links memory processing to neural dynamics, cognitive control, and conscious detection.

keywords: dynamic co activation brain states across sleep stage reveal by
topics.title: Dynamic Brain States Across Sleep
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain activity and connectivity reconfigure across sleep stages, including transitions linked to arousal, spindles, and K-complexes. It also highlights multimodal EEG-fMRI approaches to study dynamic functional organization and neurovascular coupling during human sleep.

keywords: resting state eeg alpha bold coupling
resting state fmri connectivity reflects spatial organization
topics.title: Resting-State Brain Dynamics and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on resting-state neural activity across EEG, fMRI, and MEG, emphasizing oscillatory structure, brain-state dynamics, and cortical-subcortical connectivity. It also includes normative and multi-site analyses aimed at characterizing variability across states and individuals.

keywords: local brain aging in cognitively normal
topics.title: Midlife hippocampal aging and memory
topics.description: This cluster focuses on local brain aging and hippocampal subfield differences during midlife, especially around perimenopause and menopause. It links structural measures and brain age estimations to memory and other cognitive outcomes in cognitively normal adults.

topics.title: ADHD Subtypes and Neurophysiological Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying ADHD-related subtypes and their distinct neurophysiological and brain-functional signatures. It includes resting-state EEG and fast fMRI approaches, with attention to individualized deviations, brain traffic measures, and related factors such as sleep.

keywords: brain structural connectome of adhd children
symptom dimensions show distinct nonlinear regional volume response
topics.title: ADHD Development, Connectivity, and Brain Markers
topics.description: This cluster centers on ADHD in children and youth, emphasizing developmental change, symptom dimensions, and brain structure/connectivity findings. It also includes multimodal neurobiological markers, white matter and volume correlates, and related maturation effects.

topics.title: Self-Regulation and Neuroimaging of Mindfulness
topics.description: This cluster centers on self-regulation and mindfulness-related processes measured with neuroimaging, neurofeedback, and experience sampling. The studies examine brain function, neural structure, and white matter plasticity in relation to self-modulation and mental effort.

topics.title: Neurobiological and Risk Links in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and related mood disorder outcomes, including suicidality and depressive symptoms, in relation to brain structure and white matter microstructure. It also highlights multi-omic and socioeconomic associations that may reveal neuroimmune-metabolic and biomarker signatures.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment and Behavioral Risk
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how adolescent brain development relates to behavior problems and risk factors across childhood and adolescence. It emphasizes environmental, family, and social context influences using longitudinal brain-behavior association studies.

keywords: normative modelling of brain volume for diagnostis and prognostis in multiple sclerosis
topics.title: Normative Brain Volume Modeling in Clinical Cohorts
topics.description: The cluster centers on normative modelling and longitudinal MRI analysis of brain volume in clinical cohorts, especially multiple sclerosis. It emphasizes stability, volumetric consistency, and reproducible preprocessing workflows for diagnosis and prognosis.

keywords: brain structural correlates of sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
topics.title: Normative Modeling of Glaucoma and Brain Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative modeling approaches used to relate glaucoma-related retinal and optic nerve measures to brain structure and visual cortex alterations. It also connects these findings to broader aging-brain patterns and structural correlates across MRI-based measures.

topics.title: Connectome Structure and Cortical Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on human and comparative brain organization, especially how structural connectivity, diffusion tractography, and connectome-based analyses relate to cortical microarchitecture and functional organization. It also includes atlas construction, cross-individual mapping, and developmental or genomic influences on brain network structure.

keywords: deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
topics.title: Neural Alignment for Vision and Semantics
topics.description: This cluster centers on aligning deep neural network representations with brain and behavior, especially for vision, semantic representation, and representational hierarchy. It includes work on frozen fMRI foundation embeddings, visual encoding models, and zero-transfer decoding across human brain measures.

keywords: erp correlates of medical source credibility
topics.title: EEG Methods and NeuroAI for Naturalistic Studies
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG and MEG analysis workflows, including artifact removal, denoising, cross-site variability, and reproducible large-scale neurophysiology research. It also includes applications to neuroAI, event-related potentials, and studies of motion sickness and source credibility.

topics.title: Brain Injury Imaging and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based characterization of brain injury, with emphasis on structural connectivity, microstructural alterations, and diffusion imaging. It also includes analytical tools and frameworks for assessing injury effects across human and model systems.

topics.title: Cerebrovascular Dynamics and Reactivity in fMRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cerebral blood flow, perfusion, and cerebrovascular reactivity are measured and interpreted in relation to brain function. It includes work on baseline physiology, hypoperfusion, and dynamic vascular responses across imaging methods.

keywords: multi organ relationship shared neural circuits
topics.title: Brain-Body Autonomic and Cardiorespiratory Coupling
topics.description: This cluster centers on interactions between brain, heart, and other bodily systems, with emphasis on autonomic regulation, heart rate variability, and cardiorespiratory dynamics. It also includes related work on pulsatility, cerebral perfusion, and functional connectivity changes linking neural and peripheral physiology.

keywords: a tool for transparent and standardized assessment of multisite harmonization in neuroimaging
topics.title: Head Impact, Concussion, and White Matter Change
topics.description: This cluster centers on concussion and repetitive head impact in youth contact sport athletes, with emphasis on white matter injury and recovery after collision sport exposure. It also includes neuroimaging and tractometry methods for assessing multisite harmonization and detecting subtle brain changes.

keywords: the protective role of memory suppression
topics.title: PTSD Risk and Neural Network Signatures
topics.description: This cluster focuses on post-traumatic stress disorder in relation to trauma exposure, resilience, and symptom prediction. It also highlights resting-state and network-based neural signatures, including connectivity and effective connectivity patterns.

topics.title: Reward Learning and Decision Updating
topics.description: This cluster centers on how reward prediction, uncertainty, and feedback shape learning, reversal, and choice behavior in the human brain. It also includes computational and fMRI approaches probing orbitofrontal, ventral tegmental, and neuromodulatory mechanisms.

topics.title: Adversity, resilience, and adolescent brain risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on how early life adversity and related risk or protective factors shape brain development and psychopathology in youth and adolescents. It includes work on structural brain measures, amygdala subnuclei connectivity, and hippocampal microstructure in relation to self-harm and broader mental health outcomes.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Social Cognition and Traits
topics.description: This cluster centers on how social cognitive processes such as perspective taking, self-evaluation, and social decision making relate to neural correlates and network organization. It also includes work on personality and dark-trait dimensions, including machiavellianism and psychopathic traits.

keywords: neural correlates of optimal risk taking
topics.title: Risk and Decision Neural Correlates
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and behavioral mechanisms of risky decision making, reward learning, and exploration-exploitation tradeoffs. It also includes individual differences, impulse control, and experimental paradigms such as the modified IGT and naturalistic reinforcement learning tasks.

topics.title: Social Fairness, Value, and Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how fairness perception, social inequality, and consensus making relate to value computation, reward processing, and risky decisions. It also includes developmental and clinical angles, especially adolescence and childhood trajectories, apathy, major depressive disorder, and Huntington disease.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Depression and Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression-related emotion regulation and their neural correlates across clinical and transdiagnostic contexts, including trauma, fibromyalgia, and perinatal depression. It emphasizes brain signatures, functional connectivity, and neurofunctional mechanisms underlying vulnerability and mood disorder symptoms.

keywords: graph metrics predict electroconvulsive therapy response
topics.title: Neurobiological Predictors of Depression Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and related disorders, especially how brain functional topology, connectivity, and signal variability relate to treatment outcomes. It also includes clinical interventions such as CBT, pharmacotherapy, electroconvulsive therapy, and neuromodulation, with attention to response prediction and suicidality.

topics.title: Preterm Brain Development and Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on brain structural development in preterm and medically vulnerable infants, including associations with gestational age, congenital heart disease, and postnatal age. It emphasizes developmental outcomes and long-term morphological changes across cortical, hippocampal, and subcortical regions.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Fear and Anxiety
topics.description: This cluster centers on how fear, threat, and anxiety are represented and regulated in the brain, especially involving the amygdala, prefrontal cortex, and autonomic responses. It also includes studies of fear conditioning/extinction and interventions or contexts such as neurofeedback and stressful virtual reality environments.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotions are processed and regulated in the brain, with emphasis on amygdala-related responses, cognitive reappraisal, and affective dynamics. It also includes studies of facial and emotional stimuli, as well as neural signatures and connectivity patterns underlying emotion processing.

topics.title: Personalized Neuromodulation in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and related affective symptoms, with emphasis on individualized noninvasive neuromodulation guided by prefrontal connectivity and high-field fMRI. It also links neurocircuitry, amygdala function, and reward-related features such as anhedonia and loss avoidance.

keywords: functional connectivity correlates of observed in session rumination
topics.title: Neural and Clinical Correlates of Mood Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on mood and anxiety disorders, especially major depressive disorder, and the clinical effects of treatment and symptom expression. It highlights functional connectivity, dynamic brain networks, and rumination-related cerebral mechanisms as correlates of clinical symptoms and cognitive function.

keywords: functional connectivity and genetic correlates of dimensional mood variation
topics.title: Depression Heterogeneity and Neuroimaging Correlates
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression-related disorders and their heterogeneous brain and genetic signatures across clinical and population samples. It also includes efforts to predict depression severity using rs-fMRI, connectivity measures, and cross-site machine learning approaches.

keywords: brain state dynamics during emotional reflection
topics.title: Depression Network Dynamics and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on depressive disorders, especially major depressive disorder, and their altered brain network dynamics, connectivity, and rumination-related processes. It also includes region-specific findings such as habenula-default network coupling and broader large-scale functional and structural network alterations.

keywords: magnetic field correlation imaging quantifies microstructural changes
topics.title: MRI Markers of Cortical and Iron Microstructure
topics.description: These studies focus on quantitative MRI approaches for assessing cortical lamination, myelin, and iron-related microstructure in the healthy cortex and locus coeruleus. The cluster emphasizes imaging biomarkers and laminar correspondence rather than a single disease application.

topics.title: Aging, Connectivity, and Cognitive Vulnerability
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging and disease-related factors relate to brain connectivity, functional dedifferentiation, and cognitive outcomes. It includes studies of long covid, atrial fibrillation, metabolic syndrome, and APOE-related vulnerability in older adults.

keywords: hypoperfusion loadings for outcome prediction in acute ischemic stroke
persistent gray matter hypoperfusion hemispheric cbf asymmetry
topics.title: Stroke Hypoperfusion and Outcome Prediction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on ischemic stroke, especially acute ischemic stroke, and related imaging markers such as hypoperfusion, infarct progression, and interhemispheric asymmetry. It also includes outcome prediction and longitudinal MRI measures of perivascular spaces and white matter microstructure.

topics.title: Robust Brain Morphometry Across MRI Systems
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain morphometry and segmentation methods, especially for hippocampal and subcortical structures. It also emphasizes reliability, reproducibility, and harmonisation across scanners, field strengths, and multi-site datasets.

keywords: individualized mapping of functional brain networks
topics.title: Brain Network Dynamics Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how functional and structural brain networks are organized and reorganize across adulthood and older age. It emphasizes structure-function coupling, dynamic connectivity, individualized mapping, and age- and sex-related differences in network behavior.

topics.title: White Matter Development Across Childhood
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental changes in white matter across fetal, infant, child, and adolescent stages, with attention to maturation, microstructure, and related cognitive outcomes. Several abstracts also involve preterm or neonatal populations and diffusion MRI measures of brain development.

topics.title: White Matter Diffusion and Tractography Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI measures and tractography for characterizing white matter microstructure and connectivity. It also includes comparative evaluation and reproducibility across imaging methods and multisite settings.

topics.title: Multiscale Brain Microstructure and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on multiscale characterization of brain microstructure, myelin, and connectivity using diffusion MRI, tractography, and complementary histological and transcriptomic approaches. It also includes ex vivo and postmortem human brain studies, pSOCT imaging, and atlas-based integration for anatomical reconstruction.

keywords: network level structural disconnection mapping of post stroke functional deficits
topics.title: Post-Stroke Network Disconnection and Recovery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke-related lesions and structural disconnections map onto functional deficits, including language, cognitive, and motor outcomes. It also highlights post-stroke recovery, neuroplasticity, and predictive network-based approaches across different stroke cohorts and lesion locations.

keywords: early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
the contralesional corticospinal tract aids recovery after primary motor cortex stroke
distinct effects of stroke lesion topography on multiple sensorimotor tracts
topics.title: Stroke Recovery and Motor Network Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how stroke affects motor and sensorimotor networks and how these changes relate to recovery of function. It highlights lesion topography, structural disconnection, and predictive connectivity markers for functional and visuomotor outcomes.

topics.title: Advanced MRI Methods for Brain Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on advanced MRI acquisition, reconstruction, and correction methods for brain research, with emphasis on high-field and low-field imaging, spectroscopy, and contrast-specific protocols. It also includes quality assurance, artifact handling, and applications involving deep brain stimulation and functional or structural brain assessment.

topics.title: Deep Learning for Brain MRI Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning methods for MRI analysis, including reconstruction, segmentation, and quality control. It also highlights robustness, domain adaptation, and clinical downstream applications in brain imaging.

keywords: multimodal llm base brain aging model
topics.title: Brain Aging, Vascular Risk, and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain aging research linking vascular health, neurodegeneration, and cognitive outcomes. It also includes predictive and multimodal modeling approaches for studying brain structure and aging trajectories.

topics.title: Human Cortical Cytoarchitecture and Folding Atlases
topics.description: This cluster centers on human cortical cytoarchitecture, cortical folding, and atlas-based mapping efforts. It includes major resources and datasets for brain parcellation and structural organization, especially around the Julich and BigBrain atlases.

keywords: executive control salience network structure function coupling
functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Network Reorganization in Neurological Disease
topics.description: These studies focus on how brain networks are altered in disorders such as multiple sclerosis and glioma, with attention to fatigue, cognition, and lesion-related network changes. A major theme is functional network remapping and connectivity reorganization, especially in relation to surgery and symptom prediction.

keywords: t1 weighted mri phantom for validate segmentation and cortical thickness accuracy
topics.title: Atlas-Based MRI Segmentation Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on atlas-driven MRI segmentation and registration approaches for accurate structural labeling and measurement. It includes template construction, validation phantoms, and applications to infant and human brain regions.

keywords: normative modeling of fetal cortical folding trajectories use spectral analysis of gyrification
high fidelity infant brain mri segmentation
topics.title: Fetal and Infant Brain Development Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on fetal, neonatal, and infant brain morphology, especially cortical folding, gyrification, and developmental atlas construction. It also includes segmentation and processing frameworks used to quantify brain structure across developmental stages.

keywords: comparative evaluation of latent modeling approaches
topics.title: Brain Organization Across Tasks and Naturalistic Contexts
topics.description: This cluster centers on how latent modeling and fMRI analyses characterize individual brain organization, functional organization, and cortical recruitment across task-based, rest-state, and naturalistic stimuli. The abstracts emphasize functional specialisation in the human prefrontal cortex and related circuits during behavior and social navigation.

keywords: multimodal brain connectivity integration for cognitive performance prediction in youth
topics.title: Multimodal Brain Connectivity Prediction Models
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning and graph-based approaches for integrating multimodal brain connectivity data. The studies focus on predicting phenotypes, cognitive performance, schizophrenia, and longitudinal infant brain network development using fusion and representation learning methods.

keywords: the inferior occipital lobe underlie object mnemonic discrimination
topics.title: Dynamic Memory Changes in Aging and MCI
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging and mild cognitive impairment alter episodic and mnemonic memory processes, with emphasis on dynamic brain states and related neural signatures. It also links these memory changes to specific brain substrates, including perirhinal and inferior occipital regions, and to pathology such as tau.

keywords: task derive functional connectivity captures individual differences
visuospatial attention pulvino cortical functional connectivity
topics.title: Functional Connectivity and Working Memory Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional and effective connectivity reveal individual differences in brain network organization, especially during working memory and attention tasks. It also includes network analysis approaches such as individualized parcellation, degree centrality, and Granger causality.

keywords: the landscape of dynamic functional connectivity
topics.title: Visual Hallucinations in Parkinson Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease and its progression, with emphasis on visual hallucinations and related structural, metabolic, and connectivity abnormalities. It also highlights proposed neural mechanisms, including white-matter disruption, frontal disconnection, and brainstem involvement.

topics.title: Neurodegenerative Connectivity and Cognitive Phenotypes
topics.description: The cluster centers on neurodegenerative and psychiatric conditions, especially dementia-related disorders, and their associated cognitive phenotypes. It emphasizes altered brain connectivity and resting-state network patterns as potential markers across disease states.

keywords: age related reorganization and plasticity of functional gradients in primary motor cortex
cerebellar functional topography and dynamic interactions underlie episodic memory in age
topics.title: Age-Related Brain Plasticity and Memory
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain organization and functional gradients change with age, especially in motor cortex, cerebellum, and attention networks. It also includes work linking these changes to episodic memory, executive function, and predictive modeling of memory outcomes.

topics.title: Connectome Organization and Lifespan Intelligence
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain network organization, connectivity patterns, and dynamic gradients relate to general intelligence across the lifespan. It emphasizes neurobiological signatures of cognitive diversity, maturation, and individual differences in functional brain architecture.

keywords: bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
topics.title: MRI Harmonization Methods and Evaluation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on methods for harmonizing longitudinal and multi-site MRI and neuroimaging data, including joint and Bayesian approaches. It also emphasizes evaluation tools, quality metrics, and handling site effects, missing data, and motion-related artifacts.

keywords: benchmark evaluation of functional templates for group level
topics.title: fMRI Reproducibility and Connectivity Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on fMRI analysis methods for denoising, reproducibility, and benchmark evaluation, with emphasis on multi-echo processing and functional template construction. It also includes approaches for connectivity modeling, hyperalignment, and group-level decoding.

topics.title: Aging, Cognition, and Brain Structure
topics.description: This cluster centers on cognitive ageing in healthy older adults, linking working memory, executive function, and cognitive reserve to brain structure and cortical measures. It also highlights modifiable risk factors, sleep, hormonal influences, and longevity-related factors in aging-related cognitive outcomes.

topics.title: White Matter Microstructure in Neurological Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI and related metrics used to characterize white matter and subcortical microstructure across neurological disorders. It emphasizes clinical correlates, cognitive impairment, and disease-related structural changes in conditions such as Huntington disease and Parkinson disease.

keywords: somatotopic homunculus profiles of motor cortex
topics.title: White Matter and Motor Pathway Changes
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine white matter organization and motor-related pathways across age, disease, and clinical conditions. They also use diffusion MRI, neurite metrics, and normative modeling to characterize tract alterations and cortical microstructure.

keywords: super resolution diffusion mri unveil cortical microstructural alterations
topics.title: Multimodal MRI markers in multiple sclerosis
topics.description: The cluster centers on MRI-based characterization of multiple sclerosis, especially early disease, white matter lesion burden, and cortical microstructural changes. It also links imaging findings to cognitive status, disability, and myelin-related tissue alterations.

topics.title: Brain Age Biomarkers for Neurodegeneration
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain age estimation and related imaging markers for detecting cognitive impairment, neurodegeneration, and brain atrophy. It also emphasizes normative modeling, longitudinal MRI, and biomarker discovery for diagnosis and subtype differentiation.

keywords: multiscale functional architecture of brain hubs
topics.title: Brain Hub Connectivity in Individual Differences
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how multiscale functional architecture and connectivity patterns of brain hubs relate to individual differences, stability, and predictive value in neuroscience data. It also emphasizes robustness across analytic choices, including parcellation, network modeling, and dynamic connectivity approaches.

keywords: functional brain alterations in preclinical alzheimer 's
protocol harmonized brain networks uncover key regions
topics.title: Early Alzheimer’s Brain Connectivity Changes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on functional and network-level brain alterations associated with early, preclinical, and early-onset Alzheimer’s disease. It emphasizes cognitive dysfunction, patient-specific connectivity patterns, and brain network organization changes relevant to disease classification and vulnerability.

topics.title: Neurodegenerative Subtyping in Parkinsonian Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurodegenerative disease subtyping, especially in parkinson disease and related dementia syndromes. It emphasizes substantia nigra degeneration, iron accumulation, and multimodal imaging markers for distinguishing clinical phenotypes.

topics.title: Alzheimer Biomarkers and Disease Heterogeneity
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease biology across the disease spectrum, with emphasis on amyloid, tau pathology, and biomarker progression. It also highlights heterogeneity, cognitive decline, aging, resilience, and white matter-related brain changes.

keywords: biophysical large scale brain circuit models
topics.title: fMRI-Based Brain Connectivity Modeling
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling and estimating brain connectivity and dynamics from fMRI, including intrinsic and effective connectivity, network flow, and functional integration-segregation. It also includes digital-twin and large-scale brain model approaches for explaining observable brain dynamics.

keywords: modular human ipsc derived neuronal network
topics.title: Brain Network Models of Consciousness and Anesthesia
topics.description: This cluster centers on computational brain modeling and network mechanisms used to study consciousness, propofol anesthesia, and related thalamocortical dynamics. It also includes personalized and biologically constrained modeling approaches such as virtual brain twins and iPSC-derived neuronal networks.

topics.title: Human Connectome Structure and Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on network neuroscience approaches for mapping and modeling the human connectome, including directed and multimodal representations of cortical organization. It also highlights methods for linking anatomical structure, effective hierarchies, and functional networks through modeling and community detection.

keywords: exponential distance rule base network model
topics.title: Network Control of Brain-State Transitions
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how network control theory and attractor dynamics explain transitions between brain states, including low-energy shifts and thalamic regulation of turbulent activity. It also addresses structural organization, communication efficiency, and robustness in human cortical and other neural networks.
